title: Bryophyllum uniflorum
intro: Bryophyllum uniflorum, also known as Kalanian or Life Plant, is a succulent plant species belonging to the family Crassulaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and wound-healing effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as skin irritations, burns, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Bryophyllum uniflorum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its pharmacological activities. Bryophyllum uniflorum is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical formulations.
taxonomy: Bryophyllum uniflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. It is commonly known as the dwarf alligator plant or dwarf pincushion. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. The classification of Bryophyllum uniflorum is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Rosids, Order - Saxifragales, Family - Crassulaceae, Genus - Bryophyllum, Species - B. uniflorum. Further classification may vary based on the source used.
uses: Bryophyllum uniflorum, also known as Kalanianthi, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. Its leaves are used to treat fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The plant is also used in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The anti-inflammatory properties of Bryophyllum uniflorum make it effective in reducing pain and inflammation.

parts: Bryophyllum uniflorum, commonly known as the Kalanian or ox tongue succulent, is known to be used in traditional medicine. preparations: Bryophyllum uniflorum, also known as Kalanianthe or Ox Tongue, is a succulent plant used in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations from the plant are primarily used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. side_effects: Bryophyllum uniflorum is believed to have potential side effects due to its toxic compounds, which can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and eye problems upon contact. The sap of the plant may also cause gastrointestinal issues if ingested, such as nausea and diarrhea. There is limited research available on the long-term effects of consuming Bryophyllum uniflorum, but it is advised to exercise caution when handling the plant.
